The visible compressor rotor spins continuously about the main engine axis. Keep the visible compressor rotor clipped around the center hub in the intake so the rotor remains captured on the engine axis.

A boxy candy vending cabinet with a rectangular body, a clear front panel, a dispensing shelf, and a service door at one side. The side service door rotates on a vertical hinge, the dispensing flap rotates on a horizontal hinge at the shelf opening, and a front selection knob rotates continuously about its shaft.

An illuminated classroom globe on a semicircular meridian with side tilt knobs. The sphere is supported between polar pivots inside the meridian, and the meridian is mounted to a pedestal yoke through two side trunnions with hand knobs. The globe rotates continuously about its polar axis, the meridian tilts on the horizontal trunnion axis, and the side knobs rotate on their small local axes. Keep the meridian ring clipped between the side trunnions of the pedestal yoke so the ring stays supported while tilting.

Design a retrofit/legacy-inspired Wind turbine that blends old-school construction cues with modern reliability. Keep the core as a tower with nacelle, rotor hub, and blade-root interfaces; include service hatches, bolted adapters, and pragmatic reinforcements where components meet. Rotor and blade roots should be hub-mounted with realistic load paths. The final model must be one connected mechanism where every element is structurally supported. No floating parts.

Design a retrofit/legacy-inspired Windshield sun visor that blends old-school construction cues with modern reliability. Keep the core as a visor panel on a roof hinge arm plus secondary swing pivot; include service hatches, bolted adapters, and pragmatic reinforcements where components meet. Primary and secondary pivots should be visible and mechanically credible. The final model must be one connected mechanism where every element is structurally supported. No floating parts.

An upright casino machine with a rectangular cabinet, a three-reel viewing window, a handle at the right side, and a lockable coin tray door at the base. The reels rotate continuously on parallel horizontal axes, the side handle rotates on a revolute pivot at the cabinet wall, and the coin tray door rotates downward on a bottom horizontal hinge.
A combined flatbed scanner-printer with a wide body, a scan lid on two rear barrel revolute hinges, and a front paper feed tray that slides on a prismatic joint.

category_title: Laptop stand with articulated height adjustment
prompt: A desktop laptop stand with a flat rectangular base, a single rigid top tray with two front retaining lips, and matched left and right support arms connecting the tray to the base. The side arms are counterpart supports for the same tray and stay aligned as the stand changes height. Each side arm rotates on a lower horizontal base pivot and an upper horizontal tray pivot, while the tray remains one rigid supported body.

A palm-sized camcorder with a rounded handgrip, a short front lens barrel, and a flip-out side monitor attached near the rear half of the body, plus a top mode dial behind the handle. the monitor sits on a visible side hinge block rather than blending into the body shell, and the dial remains a distinct circular control on the top shell. The side monitor rotates outward on a vertical hinge line and the lens ring rotates continuously around the barrel axis, and the top mode dial rotates continuously on its center axis. Handheld camcorder scale.

A ground-mounted tilt-deck dock plate with a flat steel platform on a central horizontal revolute hinge at a ground frame, and two support leg columns below the platform each extending on a prismatic joint to set the tilt angle.

A dual-leaf equipment hatch with two narrow doors meeting at the center of a rectangular opening. Each leaf is carried by hinges on an outer jamb, and one leaf has a rotary latch handle at the meeting edge. The two leaves rotate on separate vertical hinge axes at the side jambs, and the latch handle rotates on its own small pivot at the center seam. Keep each hatch leaf clipped into its side-jamb hinges so the paired doors stay captured through the swing.

category_title: Pedal trash bin with hinged lid
prompt: A kitchen pedal trash bin with a tall rectangular body and a rear-hinged lid. The rigid shell has a wide foot pedal projecting from the lower front face and a simple lid spanning the top opening. The lid rotates upward on a horizontal hinge at the back rim, and the pedal rotates on a transverse pivot near the base of the body.

A simple rice cooker with a cylindrical body, a domed upper lid, and a small front control panel, plus a folding carry handle spanning the upper shoulders and a power button below the front display. The body has a slightly inset lower band and a clear front latch zone, and the handle stays clearly separate from the shell surfaces. The lid rotates on a rear hinge at the rim, the carry handle rotates on side pivots at the body shoulders, and the power button moves as a separate small push control. Small household appliance scale.

A premium food processor with a broad bowl, a tall chute, a small ingredient flap in the lid, and a front panel carrying one selector dial and four program buttons. The pusher slides prismatically inside the main chute, the ingredient flap rotates on a short hinge in the lid, and the processing disc rotates continuously on the vertical shaft. The selector dial rotates continuously, and all four program buttons are separate prismatic push controls. The bowl, chute, and lid should remain clearly hollow and open above the cutter assembly. Family countertop scale.

A medical knee scooter with an adjustable knee pad post and a telescoping handlebar stem. The main frame carries two rear wheels on fixed supports and a front steering fork with two small wheels, while the knee pad column and handle stem both slide in height sleeves. The front fork yaws about the steering axis, all four wheels spin on their axles, the knee pad post slides prismatically in its support tube, and the handle stem slides prismatically in its steering column sleeve. Keep the adjustable knee-pad post clipped inside its height sleeve so the pad stays captured during vertical adjustment.

A low wide-profile metronome with a nearly flat tapered housing and a wide horizontal pendulum slot across the front face. A pendulum rod with a flat rectangular sliding weight swings through the slot from an internal center pivot; a winding key sits flush in the top face. The pendulum rotates on a revolute joint at the internal center shaft; the weight slides prismatically along the rod; the key turns continuously about the top-face axis.

A desk-post monitor arm shown without a screen, with a clamp post, a collar joint, a long primary link, a short secondary link, and a rectangular VESA head frame. The post and clamp should feel like the grounded anchor, while the links and head read as progressively lighter moving members. Make it feel like a real commercial mount with die-cast elbows, cable covers, trim caps, steel plate brackets, and practical spacing through the fold path. The collar and elbow joints revolve about supported vertical axes, and the head carries separate pan and tilt revolute joints. Keep every part mechanically grounded with no floating members, and make sure the articulation paths stay clear so nothing clips through covers, stops, housings, rails, or neighboring links.

A compact training pump with a low base, a shorter barrel, a T-handle, and a round gauge integrated into the base frame, plus a small pressure-release button on the gauge housing. the shorter proportions still keep the main pumping motion obvious, and the button is clearly separate from the gauge body. The handle and rod slide prismatically along the barrel axis and the gauge needle rotates on a central pivot in the gauge face, and the pressure-release button moves prismatically as a small push control. Compact floor-pump scale.

category_title: Dishwasher with dropdown door and sliding racks
prompt: A pro-style stainless dishwasher with a deep dropdown door, a tall upper rack, a broad lower rack, and a front control fascia carrying a large cycle knob, a start button, and a delayed-start button. The door rotates on a bottom revolute hinge, the upper and lower racks slide prismatically on heavy side rails, and the two spray arms rotate continuously on central vertical supports in the tub. The large knob rotates continuously, both visible buttons are separate small prismatic push buttons, and the detergent-cup lid on the inner door opens on a short hinge. Keep the tub, door liner, and rack wirework visibly hollow and open. Full domestic kitchen scale.

A city scooter with a folding stem and folding handle grips for storage. The narrow deck carries a rear wheel at the tail, the front wheel mounts in a steerable fork, and the T-bar has short grip sections hinged at the ends. The front fork yaws about the steering axis, both wheels spin on their axles, the stem rotates on its folding hinge near the deck, and the two grip sections rotate on hinge axes at the bar ends. Keep the folding handle grips clipped to the bar-end hinges so the grip sections stay attached in both folded and riding positions.

category_title: Top-load washing machine with hinged lid
prompt: An impeller-style top-load washer with a broad tub opening, a hinged glass lid, a detergent drawer in the top deck, and a softener cup lid on the center wash plate. The main lid rotates upward on a rear hinge, the detergent drawer slides prismatically from the top surface, the wash basket rotates continuously on the vertical axis, and the softener cup lid rotates on a small hinge at the center hub. A selector dial rotates continuously on the rear console, and three nearby buttons are separate prismatic push controls. The tub opening should remain visibly hollow and deep. Full domestic scale.

A toy hatchback car with a short nose, a tall passenger cabin, a rear liftgate, and four small wheels in open arches, plus an opening hood over the front compartment. the doors and rear hatch remain visibly separate from the body shell, and the hood remains a separate top panel over the front fenders. The four wheels rotate continuously on their axle shafts, the side doors rotate on vertical hinges, and the rear liftgate rotates upward on a top horizontal hinge, and the hood rotates upward on a short front-compartment hinge line. Handheld toy-vehicle scale.

A low console joystick module with a shallow circular base cup, an outer gimbal ring, an inner trunnion cradle, and a short central stick capped by a knurled collar. The base should feel panel-mounted and the nested supports should fully explain the motion without relying on a decorative outer shell. Make it feel like a real industrial control with cast yokes, machined pivots, trim bezels only where needed, and believable thickness around the gimbal rings. The outer ring revolves about a left-to-right axis through side pivots, and the inner cradle revolves about an orthogonal front-to-back axis around the same control center. Keep every part mechanically grounded with no floating members, and make sure the articulation paths stay clear so nothing clips through covers, stops, housings, rails, or neighboring links.

A deep canopy range hood with a front rail carrying five separate buttons and two hinged baffle-filter panels under the intake. The five buttons depress independently as small prismatic controls, and the two filter panels rotate downward on their rear hinge lines inside the canopy. Professional kitchen hood scale.

A four-burner gas stove top with a flat inset surface and five front rotary knobs aligned along the lower edge. The five knobs rotate continuously on their front-facing shafts below the burner field. Countertop cooktop scale.

A swing-arm desk lamp with a round base, a long lower arm, a shorter upper arm, and a drum shade. The lower arm rotates on a hinge at the base collar, the upper arm rotates on a hinge at the elbow, and the drum shade tilts on a hinge at the tip yoke. Reading-desk lamp scale.

A gaming desk with a wide surface, a cable management pull-out tray, and a single pedestal drawer. The cable tray slides prismatically under the front desk edge. The pedestal drawer slides prismatically on its own rails.

A premium countertop stand mixer with a broad heel, a medium bowl on a low slide carriage, and a tidy rounded head fitted with a whisk. Keep the golden articulation tree intact: base to bowl short prismatic slide, base to head rear revolute tilt hinge, head to whisk continuous vertical spin, base speed control as a revolute side control, and base head-lock as a short prismatic button.

A triple-head ceiling light fixture with a compact canopy, three curved arms, and cylindrical spots on small yokes. Each arm rotates on a hinge at the canopy body, and each spot tilts on a horizontal hinge between its yoke arms. Kitchen ceiling-light scale.

A bedside nightstand with a small square wooden body on four legs, one shallow top drawer on prismatic wooden slide runners with a small round knob handle, and a lower open shelf on fixed rails below the drawer.

A vacuum blender with a broad base, a pitcher, and a hinged vacuum cap with a small release button at the top. The cap rotates upward on a rear hinge over the pitcher opening, the release button depresses as a small prismatic control, and the blade spins continuously at the bottom of the pitcher. Premium kitchen appliance scale.

A long white telephoto prime with a removable rotating tripod foot collar. The tripod collar ring rotates on a revolute joint about the lens axis. A focus ring near the front of the barrel rotates on a separate revolute joint.

A three-joint articulated reading floor lamp with a round base and three sequential link sections ending in a shade. Each link connects to the next on a revolute elbow joint. The shade tilts on a revolute joint at the tip.

An aluminium extension ladder with a sliding fly section and a hinged stand-off bar spanning the top of the upper section. The fly section moves prismatically along the base rails, and the stand-off bar rotates upward on side hinges at the top of the fly. Exterior wall-access scale.

A square box fan with a low frame stand, a side tilt adjustment knob, and a top service flap over the motor housing. The main housing tilts on horizontal side pivots, the fan blade spins continuously on its central shaft, the adjustment knob rotates continuously, and the service flap rotates upward on a short top hinge. Workshop cooling scale.

A compact bench drill press with a column, a tilting table, and a front rack pinion with a fold-out crank handle. The table slides prismatically on the column, the table rotates on a horizontal hinge at the support collar, and the crank handle rotates continuously on the lift shaft while its grip rotates on its small local axle. Bench tool scale.

A trackball desktop controller with a wide rectangular housing and a large spherical trackball in a circular socket on the top face. The trackball rotates continuously about a horizontal axis in its socket. A scroll wheel on the side face rotates continuously about a horizontal axis.

A small hobby vise with a sliding front jaw, a suction base lever, and a screw handle at the front. The jaw slides prismatically on the guide, the screw handle rotates continuously on the lead-screw axis, and the suction lever rotates on a side pivot at the base. Small workbench scale.

A swivel bar stool. A pedestal base rises to a seat post and seat pan, with an optional foot ring fixed to the post. Joints: the seat rotates continuously about the vertical pedestal axis. Emphasize a long vertical proportion with a small head or hub at the top.

A studio spotlight on a yoke stand. A stand carries a U-shaped yoke, and the lamp can sits between the yoke arms. Joints: the yoke pans on a vertical revolute base, and the lamp tilts on a horizontal revolute axle. Emphasize a long vertical proportion with a small head or hub at the top.

A compact purifier with a hinged lower front panel and a small top rocker switch. The lower front panel rotates outward on a side hinge, the internal filter slides prismatically out from the lower opening, and the rocker switch pivots on a short local hinge in the top panel. Small room scale.

An artist's single-post adjustable easel with a round base, a telescoping center post, and a tilt cradle at the top. The center post extends prismatically from the base tube. The canvas tilt cradle tilts on a revolute hinge at the post top.

A wall thermostat with a large dial ring, a lower row of three buttons, and a hinged top setup flap. The dial ring rotates continuously about the center axis, the three lower buttons depress independently, and the top setup flap rotates upward on a short top hinge. Residential thermostat scale.

A countertop toaster oven. A rectangular oven body carries a front door, side walls, and a control panel with shaft-mounted knobs. Joints: the door is revolute about a horizontal front hinge axis, and each knob rotates about its own control shaft. Add one fixed stop flange or secondary frame lip around the opening.

A device mounted on a tripod. Three legs meet at a tripod crown, which supports a head and a device plate. Joints: each leg folds on a revolute hinge at the crown, the head pans about vertical, and the device tilts on a horizontal revolute axis. Emphasize a long vertical proportion with a small head or hub at the top.

A single-axis yaw turntable. A round base supports a rotating upper plate on a centered hub and bearing stack, with a small payload bracket above. The upper plate is one revolute joint about the vertical center axis.

A drawer-like instrument slide with a fixed base channel, a traveling carriage tray, and a blocky front stop feature. The base and carriage should read as one clean guided pair with enough overlap that the moving tray never feels unsupported. Make it feel real with machined guide faces, wear strips, stop blocks, and grounded plate thickness throughout the base and carriage. The carriage moves on a single prismatic axis along the channel direction. Keep every part mechanically grounded with no floating members, and make sure the articulation paths stay clear so nothing clips through covers, stops, housings, rails, or neighboring links.

A folding pedestrian barrier with a wall-anchor bracket and three rectangular panel sections linked in a Z-fold chain. The first panel pivots on a revolute joint at the wall bracket; each subsequent panel connects to the previous on a revolute hinge at the folding edge, allowing the barrier to extend across an opening or fold flat against the wall.

A heavy strap hinge with two long leaf plates, alternating barrel knuckles, and a prominent hinge pin along one edge. The leaves should feel thick and load-bearing rather than like decorative sheet metal, with the knuckles occupying real mechanical volume. Make it feel real with formed plate edges, machined pin ends, washers, and believable leaf thickness relative to the barrel diameter. One revolute joint connects the two leaves along the barrel axis. Keep every part mechanically grounded with no floating members, and make sure the articulation paths stay clear so nothing clips through covers, stops, housings, rails, or neighboring links.

A vertical labeling platen with a cylindrical base housing, a central spindle, and a broad round top plate for holding work. The top plate should dominate the silhouette while the support stays compact underneath. One continuous revolute joint spins the spindle and top plate about the vertical centerline.

A sensor roll module with a short pedestal, coaxial side supports, and a narrow instrument barrel mounted between the supports. The geometry should stay compact and dense so the barrel, supports, and grounded base all read as one believable subassembly. Push the realism with bearing cartridges, flange rings, local trim covers, and grounded proportions throughout the fixed frame. The barrel rotates as one revolute joint about its longitudinal axis through the support pair. Keep every part mechanically grounded with no floating members, and make sure the articulation paths stay clear so nothing clips through covers, stops, housings, rails, or neighboring links.

A tilt head with two side towers, a horizontal cross-shaft, and a rectangular payload cradle suspended between the towers. The towers should visibly carry the pivot so the motion reads as a supported tilt mechanism. One revolute joint pitches the cradle about the horizontal shaft axis.

A CDJ digital turntable with a rectangular housing, a round jog wheel on the top panel, and a tilting display bezel. The jog wheel rotates continuously about its vertical axis. The display panel tilts on a revolute hinge at the display's bottom edge.
A three-lobe spinner with a triangular bearing hub and three rounded lobe arms, spinning freely on a central revolute axle.

A folding travel quadcopter with a rectangular body and four radial arms that fold back alongside the body. Each arm folds on a revolute hinge at the arm-to-body joint about a vertical axis. Each motor propeller rotates continuously about the motor's vertical axis.

A street single-post floodlight with a round tapered pole on a square base plate, an outreach curved arm at the pole top, and a wide rectangular LED flood head tilting on a horizontal revolute joint at the arm tip.

A saddle-body pan-tilt unit. A grounded housing supports a lower rotary stage and a compact upper tilt cradle with a small output plate. The lower stage is a yaw revolute joint and the upper cradle is a pitch revolute joint.

A steer axle with kingpin stub-axles: a wide I-beam center beam, two kingpin knuckle assemblies pivoting on vertical revolute joints at the beam ends for steering, a track rod connecting the two steering arms, and disc-brake hubs spinning on revolute joints at the knuckle spindle faces.

A three-joint robotic arm for pick-and-place work. A pedestal base carries a shoulder housing, upper arm link, forearm link, and compact wrist head. Joints: shoulder revolute about vertical, elbow revolute about horizontal, wrist revolute about the forearm axis. Keep the wrist or end section very compact relative to the upstream links.

A screw-in light bulb seated in a socket. A bulb envelope sits above a threaded metal base, and the socket body holds the mating threaded collar. Joints: the bulb rotates continuously about the shared socket axis. Favor thick collars, shoulders, or end caps around the axis.

A screw-cap bottle. A bottle body rises to a threaded neck, capped by a separate screw closure. Joints: the cap rotates continuously about the bottle neck axis. Favor thick collars, shoulders, or end caps around the axis.

A large Dobsonian reflector on an alt-az rocker box. A wide cylindrical mirror cell and open truss-tube assembly carry the primary mirror at the base. The tube assembly pivots on revolute altitude trunnion joints inside the rocker box side walls. The rocker box rotates on a continuous azimuth bearing on a flat ground board.

A robotic leg module for a walking machine. An upper leg housing carries a thigh link, shank link, and compact ankle-foot section. Joints: hip revolute about a lateral axis, knee revolute about a lateral axis, ankle revolute about a lateral axis. Favor boxy joint housings and thick links over thin shells.

category_title: Sewing box with simple hinged lid
prompt: A small sewing box with a plain top lid. A rectangular box body supports one lid panel along the rear edge. Joints: the lid is a single revolute panel about a horizontal rear hinge axis. Favor thick side walls and a heavy frame around the moving panel.

category_title: Roof vent tower with hinged flap
prompt: A rooftop vent tower with a weather flap. An upright vent housing ends in a framed outlet, with one flap attached along the top edge. Joints: the flap is a single revolute panel about a horizontal top-edge axis. Favor thick side walls and a heavy frame around the moving panel.
A concrete bell tower with a plain rectangular shaft and a large bell on a steel frame yoke swinging on a revolute joint.

A heavy industrial factory-rated four-wing revolving door with solid steel panel wings. A thick central post carries four wide solid rectangular steel wings. The wing assembly rotates continuously about the vertical axis.

category_title: Clock tower with rotating hour and minute hands
prompt: A monolith obelisk clock tower with a square tapering granite shaft on a plinth base, a clock face inset on each of the four shaft faces at the upper third with hour and minute hands on concentric revolute joints at each face hub.

A city commuter fork with a straight round-blade steel unicrown, fender mounts at the blade tips, and a threadless straight steerer clamped in a riser stem. Wide flat city handlebars with ergonomic grip flares clamp in the stem at a center pinch collar. Articulate all moving parts with physical plausibility.

A single-sash sliding window. A rectangular outer frame contains one fixed frame segment and one sliding sash. Joints: the sash translates prismatically along horizontal guide channels. Use a low wide frame with the moving member spanning most of the opening.

A hinged service access panel on an equipment face. A rectangular frame surrounds the opening, and a panel door hangs from one side with a latch edge opposite. Joints: the door is a single revolute panel about a vertical side hinge axis. Favor thick side walls and a heavy frame around the moving panel.

A sliding security gate. A fixed post and frame support one gate leaf captured by a top track or bottom guide. Joints: the gate leaf translates prismatically along the track axis. Use a low wide frame with the moving member spanning most of the opening.
A service elevator with a large steel car on a vertical prismatic joint, a collapsible accordion gate on a scissor linkage, and a solid inner door on a revolute hinge.

A single-leaf drawbridge. A fixed shore frame carries one bridge leaf on heavy side bearings. Joints: the bridge leaf is a single revolute panel about a horizontal shore-side axis. Use a low, wide body with the moving panel spanning most of the width.

A tandem stoker crankset with a wide rectangular bottom-bracket shell, two crank arms on a hollow steel spindle on a revolute axle, a single small 32T sprocket chainring on the right crank arm, and flat composite platform pedals spinning on revolute stub axles at each arm tip.

A twin-mast pallet-lift carriage. A grounded mast frame uses two uprights and a crosshead to carry a boxy carriage with visible guide shoes at each side. The carriage moves on one vertical prismatic joint.

A lab fixture XY slide block. A grounded lower guide carries a broad carriage, and the carriage supports a transverse upper slide with a compact top deck. The lower and upper axes are orthogonal prismatic joints.

A portal-lite dual-rail gantry. A grounded two-rail chassis carries a transverse beam riding on end trucks, and the beam supports a central runner. The beam translates prismatically along the base rails, and the runner translates prismatically along the beam axis.

A pedestal offset rotary head. A lower rotary platter supports a side-mounted column, and the column carries a smaller upper turntable displaced from the lower axis. The lower platter and upper turntable are independent revolute joints about separate supported axes.

A skateboard with standard trucks. A deck supports front and rear truck hangers, each holding one axle and two wheels. Joints: each wheel spins continuously about its axle, and each truck steers on a revolute kingpin axis. Favor thick forks, stout axle blocks, and chunky frame sections.

A drawer-slide style two-stage extension. A fixed outer channel carries a large moving member, and the large member carries a smaller output member with a plain face. The moving members are serial prismatic joints.

A flush-mount panel board with a rectangular steel frame recessed in a wall box, a main hinged door on two barrel revolute hinges at the left edge, and a secondary inner cover panel on a revolute hinge at the top edge for inner circuit-breaker row access.

category_title: CCTV mast with pan-tilt camera head
prompt: A broadcast-camera column with a wide square base plate, a round steel pole, a motorized pan-tilt head at the pole top with a vertical revolute pan axis and a horizontal revolute tilt axis, and a box camera housing mounted on the tilt axis output.

A ceiling fan with a light kit: a five-blade fan on a central revolute axle with a cylindrical motor housing on a standard downrod, and an integrated lower light bowl with a hinged glass diffuser cover that swings open on two side revolute hinge pins for bulb access.

A searchlight tower with a pan-tilt lamp head. A mast support carries a yoke, and the lamp body sits between the yoke arms. Joints: the head pans on a vertical revolute base and tilts on a horizontal revolute yoke axis. Favor large bearing housings and thick side supports around the rotary stage.

A hand syringe. A cylindrical barrel holds a plunger rod and plunger head, ending in a nozzle tip. Joints: the plunger translates prismatically along the barrel axis. Use a low wide frame with the moving member spanning most of the opening.

A folding A-frame step ladder. A front climbing frame and a rear support frame meet at a top cap, with wide treads on the front frame. Joints: the rear frame folds relative to the front frame on a single revolute hinge at the top cap. Favor thick rails, broad feet, and a chunky top bracket.

A stick vacuum with a folding wand. A motor body connects to a straight wand and a floor head, with the fold joint placed between the body and the wand. Joints: the fold joint is revolute about a horizontal axis, and the floor head pitches on a second revolute hinge. Favor boxy joint housings and thick links over thin shells.

A boxed frame vane array. A grounded rectangular housing supports several evenly spaced rigid vanes, each carried on its own pair of end supports. The vanes are independent revolute joints about their long supported axes.

A compact lift-mast fork module. Two upright columns tied by a crosshead support a moving fork carriage with clear wraparound guides at each side. One prismatic joint lifts the carriage along the mast axis.

A wafer-style coaxial stage stack. A broad lower platter supports two thinner upper decks on a common shaft, and each deck remains visually distinct as its own rigid stage. The three decks are independent revolute joints about the shared vertical axis.

A triple-box telescoping slide. A grounded outer channel carries a middle box member, and the middle member carries an inner box member. The middle and inner members translate on serial prismatic joints.

category_title: Linear carriage with independent rotary end-effector
prompt: A crossbeam carriage with roll head. A grounded beam rail carries a short carriage, and the carriage carries a small roll spindle in a side-supported bearing block. The carriage translates on a prismatic joint along the beam, and the spindle rotates on a separate revolute joint about its shaft axis.

A machine-table XYZ slide stack. A grounded lower X guide carries a broad carriage, the carriage carries a Y slide, and the Y slide carries a vertical Z stage. The X, Y, and Z motions are three supported prismatic joints.

A box-guide two-stage extension chain. A grounded outer channel carries a middle runner, and the middle runner carries a slim inner runner with a plain end face. The runners are two serial prismatic joints.

A three-arm turnstile gate. A central column supports a rotor hub with three radial arms inside a fixed frame. Joints: the arm hub rotates continuously about the vertical center axis. Favor large bearing housings and thick side supports around the rotary stage.

A marine porro-prism binocular with wide sealed objective barrels in a rubber-armored housing, two barrels on a central revolute hinge, a center focus wheel on a revolute joint, and a built-in compass housing that rotates on a revolute joint set into the right barrel body.

A tenkeyless gaming keyboard with a media roller and rear support feet. The shallow rectangular case carries a dense key grid and a small roller at the upper right corner, with two folding feet under the back edge. The keycaps move as short vertical prismatic plungers, the media roller rotates on its axis, and the rear feet rotate on their small hinge axes.

A record turntable. A plinth supports a central platter and a tonearm base off to one side. Joints: the platter rotates continuously about vertical, and the tonearm swings on a revolute pivot at the tonearm base. Favor large bearing housings and thick side supports around the rotary stage.

A twin-rail parallel gripper head. A grounded central body carries a left slide jaw and a right slide jaw on mirrored guides, each ending in a tall rigid finger. The two jaw slides are independent prismatic joints along the common closing axis.

A pan-base Z-slide module. A fixed lower rotary table carries an upright guide frame with a compact moving head. The rotary table is a revolute joint about vertical, and the head is a prismatic joint on the upright guide.

category_title: Watch winder box with hinged lid
prompt: A watch winder presentation box. A box body supports a top lid and one internal cradle mounted on a small spindle. Joints: the lid is revolute about a horizontal rear hinge axis, and the cradle rotates continuously about its spindle. Use a low, wide body with the moving panel spanning most of the width.

A pedestal-mounted tracking dish with a broad reflector and twin feed supports. The dish back frame is carried by a rear fork on top of a rotating column, and a small locking knob sits on the elevation side plate. The column rotates about a vertical azimuth axis, the dish frame rotates about a horizontal elevation axis within the fork, and the locking knob rotates on its own local axis. Keep the broad reflector frame clipped within the rear fork trunnions so the dish stays captured while elevating.

category_title: Desktop monitor with tilt swivel stand
prompt: A premium desktop monitor with a thin display shell, a broad stand column, and a heavy base plate, plus a small bank of menu buttons under the lower bezel. the stand head keeps enough depth for a real tilt and pivot assembly behind the screen, and the controls are separate parts instead of one fused panel. The screen housing tilts on a horizontal hinge at the stand head, rotates continuously about its central viewing axis for portrait mode, and the stand swivels continuously on a vertical axis at the base, and the menu buttons articulate independently as small prismatic push controls. Professional desk-monitor scale.

A vertical-and-sideways stage block. A grounded mast supports a compact moving head, and the moving head supports a short transverse slide. The stage uses one vertical prismatic joint and one horizontal sideways prismatic joint.

category_title: Tackle box with simple hinged lid
prompt: A tackle box with a plain hinged lid. A rectangular box body supports one lid panel along the rear edge, with internal tray details kept fixed inside the body. Joints: the lid is a single revolute panel about a horizontal rear hinge axis. Favor thick side walls and a heavy frame around the moving panel.
A wood-fired oven housing with a steel mouth surround and a thick steel door that hinges down on a bottom revolute axle with a forged bar handle.

A naval ship-deck truck cannon with a short fat cast-iron barrel on trunnion revolute joints in the side cheeks of a wheeled wooden truck carriage, four small solid wheels on revolute axles at the carriage corners, and a wooden quoin wedge that slides on a prismatic joint below the barrel breech for elevation.

A cantilever XZ positioning head. A fixed horizontal rail carries a carriage that supports a short vertical column with a moving tool plate. The rail carriage and the vertical tool plate are orthogonal prismatic joints.
A retro beige desktop tower with a hinged side panel on a revolute joint and a 5.25-inch drive bay with a sliding tray and a flip-open bezel on a revolute hinge.

A professional camera flash with a tall rear control body, a broad mounting foot, and a forward-facing head between short yoke arms, plus a bounce card sliding from the head top. the yoke makes the tilt axis visible on both sides of the head, and the bounce card remains a distinct insert inside the head shell. A nested wrist head with an outer yaw ring, an inner pitch cradle, and a central roll cartridge projecting forward. A desk fan with a tilting head. A base supports a guarded fan head between two side brackets. Joints: the fan rotor spins continuously about its axle, and the head tilts on a horizontal revolute axis through the side brackets. Use a low wide support with the rotating mass kept near the ground or mounting plane.

A tall household juicer with a tapered base body, a clear upper chamber lid, and a long feed pusher moving vertically through an offset chute near one side of the top. The feed pusher slides prismatically within the chute, the lid rotates on a rear horizontal hinge, the three control buttons move on short prismatic plungers, the locking latch rotates on a small side pivot, and the internal filter basket rotates continuously about the vertical axis.

category_title: Adjustable weight bench with hinged backrest
prompt: An adjustable decline bench with a long back pad, an articulated seat, and a front leg-roller assembly, plus a front carry handle between the wheels. the frame has a low wheel set at one end for moving the bench around the room, and the handle projects from the front crossmember without floating. The back pad rotates on a central horizontal hinge, the seat rotates on its own hinge ahead of it, the paired leg rollers spin continuously on their support axle, and the transport wheels rotate continuously on the frame axle. A mini tower crane with a round mast, a short horizontal jib rotating on a vertical revolute axis, and a cable hoist drum at the jib root.

category_title: Desktop pencil sharpener with hand crank and drawer
prompt: A compact office pencil sharpener with a rounded shell, a folding side crank, a lower pullout tray, and a short mode slider beside the pencil port. A nested three-carriage linear chain. A fixed guide frame carries a large carriage, the large carriage carries a medium carriage, and the medium carriage carries a small output carriage. The carriages form three serial prismatic joints along one common axis.

category_title: Standing desk with synchronous telescoping legs and articulated controls
prompt: A compact home-office standing desk with a narrow top, twin telescoping legs, and a small side control pod under one corner, plus a paddle-style controller with separate up and down paddles. the top frame remains visibly separate from the lifting columns, and the two paddles remain visibly split instead of reading as one rocker strip. The two leg stages slide prismatically within their outer columns as a synchronized pair, and the up and down paddles rotate on short local pivots in the controller body. Small home-office desk scale.

A survey mast with rotary top plate. A grounded outer mast carries nested lift tubes, and the top tube carries a shallow pan head with a square face. The lift tubes are serial prismatic joints, and the pan head is a revolute joint about the vertical mast axis.

A rehabilitation overbed table with a U-shaped mobile base, a single mast, a split-top work surface, and a side tilt-release handle, plus a smaller reading wing beside the main top. the smaller side wing shares the same support head as the main top instead of floating beside it, and the secondary wing is mounted on a short support bracket at the tray edge. The mast slides prismatically for height adjustment, the main top rotates on a horizontal hinge at the head, and the four base casters spin continuously on their axles, and the reading wing rotates on its own short hinge beside the main top. Therapy-room table scale.

A platform cart with a fold-flat rear handle and a front deck extension. The main deck rides on four caster assemblies, the rear handle hinges down over the deck, and a secondary platform panel slides out from under the front edge. Each caster wheel spins on its axle and swivels on a vertical stem, the rear handle rotates on its hinge pivots, and the front extension slides prismatically along its guide rails. Keep the front deck extension clipped on its guide rails so the secondary platform stays attached while sliding outward.

A split-palm dual finger mechanism. A rectangular palm block carries left and right phalanx chains on separate root pivots, and each finger ends in a blunt tip pad. Each finger bends through three revolute joints in one plane, and the two fingers stay mechanically independent.

A student monocular microscope with a rectangular base, a curved support arm, and a slide stage with a projecting carriage, plus a second stage-axis control on the stage side. the optical head is carried by a visible vertical guide instead of a hidden internal linkage, and the two axis controls sit as distinct coaxial knobs on the stage bracket. The head carriage slides prismatically on the arm column, the objective turret rotates continuously, and the stage carriage slides prismatically across the stage, and the second stage control rotates continuously on its local axis. Classroom microscope scale.

A USB drive with a swivel cover. A compact body carries the connector, and a U-shaped cover wraps around the body on a side pin. Joints: the cover rotates continuously about the side pivot axis. Use a short, wide proportion with the rotating element dominating the front face.

A compact remote weapon station with a small gun cradle, a sensor box, and a manually adjustable shield flap. The rotating base supports the elevation cradle between two side supports, and a narrow shield flap is hinged at the top edge of the front armor. The base rotates about a vertical azimuth axis, the weapon cradle rotates about a horizontal elevation axis, and the shield flap rotates on its own top hinge axis. Keep the adjustable shield flap clipped to its top hinge on the front armor so the flap stays attached while moving.

A surveillance blimp with a long envelope, a small sensor gondola, and side-mounted engine nacelles aft of the cabin, plus vectoring engine pylons beside the gondola. the tail assembly remains visibly separate with distinct vertical and horizontal control surfaces, and the engine pods stay visibly carried by those pylons. The engine propellers spin continuously on their local axes, the rudder rotates on its vertical hinge, and the elevators rotate on horizontal tail hinges, and the engine pods rotate on short pivots at the pylon ends. A portable rolling tool chest with a ribbed plastic shell, a telescoping pull handle at the back, and a hinged top lid over the main compartment, plus a front push-button latch. the lower body stays deep enough to read as a real rolling chest rather than a suitcase, and the latch is a visibly separate part in the lid nose. The pull handle slides prismatically in its rear channels, the main lid rotates on a rear hinge line, and the two rear wheels spin continuously on their axle, and the latch button translates prismatically into the lid shell. Garage storage scale.

A compact karaoke speaker with a telescoping trolley handle, rear transport wheels, and a top deck carrying a large knob and two mode buttons, plus a power rocker and two menu buttons. the telescoping rails are clearly visible behind the upper shell, and the rocker stays distinct from the two smaller buttons beside it. The trolley handle slides prismatically on its twin rails, the rear wheels spin continuously on their axle, and the main knob rotates continuously, and the power rocker rotates on a short pivot and the two menu buttons each depress independently. A traditional windmill with a rotating sail hub. A tower body supports a cap and a front hub carrying multiple lattice blades. Joints: the blade hub rotates continuously about the windmill shaft axis, and the cap rotates about the vertical tower axis. Use a low wide support with the rotating mass kept near the ground or mounting plane.

A dual-slot graphics card with one large center fan and one smaller side fan in an asymmetrical shroud. The flat card body has a rectangular PCB and heatsink slab, with the two fan openings offset along the length and the I O bracket at one end. Both fan rotors spin continuously about their local axes within the fixed shroud. Keep both fan rotors clipped to their center hubs inside the asymmetrical shroud so they remain captured during rotation.

A rail-slide with hinged nose. A grounded rail supports a sliding truck, and the truck supports a compact hinged nose link. The truck translates on a prismatic joint, and the nose link rotates on a supported revolute joint.

A plunger-and-tab mechanism. A grounded housing guides a straight plunger, and the plunger nose meets a short hinged tab at the front face. The plunger is a prismatic joint and the tab is a revolute joint.

A swing-beam carriage module. A fixed column carries a rigid beam that can sweep around the column, and a compact carriage rides along the beam. The beam is a revolute joint about the column support, and the carriage is a prismatic joint along the beam.

A trunnion-root elbow arm. A grounded pedestal supports an upper link, a forearm, and a plain end plate, with the shoulder hub visibly larger than the elbow hub. The shoulder and elbow are serial revolute joints about parallel supported axes.

A nested rectangular telescoping boom. A grounded root support carries an outer box section, a middle section, and smaller inner sections that remain visibly nested. Each nested section slides on a prismatic joint along the common boom axis.
A laminated-glass sunroof cassette with a narrow aluminium frame and a glass panel that slides fully rearward on twin prismatic guide rails.

A modern concrete lighthouse with a square tower and a compact glass lantern at the top. An exterior storm shutter panel with wider slats and a stout outer frame. A decorative arched-top mailbox with a front flap door and a side flag. The front door rotates on a horizontal lower hinge axis, and the side flag rotates on its own small axis. A playground bench swing with a short backrest and rigid side hangers. The bench seat is carried below a top beam by two side link pairs, one on each side, so the bench stays level as it moves. A low-profile display lazy susan with a smooth round top and a knurled outer rim. The upper disc sits just above a thin fixed base ring with a compact bearing hidden between them. A commuter folding scooter with a long deck, a handlebar stem that collapses on a revolute hinge at the base, and front and rear wheels on revolute axles.

A small pet door insert with a lightweight flap and a vertical security cover stored at one side. A machine-bed portal gantry. Two side uprights tied by a traveling beam support a central carriage, and the carriage supports a vertical head with a plain faceplate. The beam, carriage, and vertical head are prismatic joints along their supported axes.

A desktop Wi-Fi router with four external antennas and a row of front buttons. A pickup tailgate with a center pass-through door built into the main panel. A stationary exercise bike. A floor frame supports the flywheel housing, crank set, seat post, and handlebar mast. A cantilever garden gate with a slatted aluminum leaf and a rear counterbalance tail. A boxed three-link hinge chain. A fixed root clevis supports four rigid members in series ending in a simple output pad. Three serial revolute joints connect the members about supported parallel pin axes.

A tower-mounted cantilever tooling arm. A short tower supports a long first link, a shorter second link, and a narrow wrist cartridge ending in a plain faceplate. The arm uses three serial revolute joints carried by the tower, upper link, and forelink.

A side-cheek articulated finger chain. A root block carries three rigid finger links of decreasing length, with every knuckle supported by paired cheeks and a visible pin barrel. The links are joined by three serial revolute joints in one plane.

A boxed four-joint revolute chain. A grounded root bracket carries five rigid links in series with long side plates and a compact end tab at the tip. The chain uses four serial revolute joints about parallel supported axes in one plane.

A wheelbarrow. A tray sits between two handles, with one front wheel in a fork and two resting legs at the rear. Joints: the front wheel rotates continuously about its axle. Favor thick forks, stout axle blocks, and chunky frame sections.

A task-light style folding arm. A fixed root bracket supports three rigid links connected by offset clevis pivots and ending in a plain tip pad. Three revolute joints let the arm fold back near the root and extend outward in one plane.

A swing-arm extension chain. A grounded support carries a single arm link, and the arm link carries a compact telescoping nose slide. The arm rotates on a revolute joint at the root, and the nose slide is a prismatic joint.

A upright lift slide with wrist plate. A fixed mast supports a moving carriage, and the carriage supports a short hinged plate with a plain front face. The carriage moves on a vertical prismatic joint, and the plate rotates on a supported revolute hinge.

A tall pantry cabinet with separate upper and lower doors on the same side. A top-freezer refrigerator with two stacked front doors. The tall cabinet has a smaller upper freezer compartment and a larger lower fresh-food compartment, each with its own side-hinged door. The upper and lower doors rotate on separate vertical hinge axes at the cabinet side.

A ladder-frame multi-segment fold-out arm. A grounded base plate carries four short boxed links ending in a small platform bracket, with the inner links intentionally deeper than the outer ones. The arm uses four serial revolute joints about parallel axes in one plane.

A yaw-shoulder wristed arm. A grounded base pedestal supports an upper arm, a forearm, and a short wrist link ending in a tool flange. The shoulder, elbow, and wrist are three serial revolute joints with the wrist carried at the tip of the forearm.

A pair of cat-eye eyeglasses with a swept upper rim and slim temples. The rigid front frame carries two hinge blocks at the outer corners and small adjustable nose-pad arms near the bridge. A masonry-supported overshot waterwheel with a small drive pulley and a chute gate. category_title: Branching tree with two independent rotary branches
prompt: A mast-and-outrigger two-branch rotary head. A short mast rises from a heavy foot and supports two unequal branch arms on separate collar blocks, one longer and one shorter. Each branch rotates on its own revolute support axis with no coupling between them.

A violin case with a shaped lower shell and a matching top lid. The rigid lower shell carries the instrument bed, and the lid is attached along one long rear edge with a carry handle at the front. A offset clevis lever linkage. A fixed root clevis carries three simple lever members with stepped lengths and a compact terminal tab. The linkage uses three serial revolute joints about parallel pin axes.

A two-link arm with output slide. A fixed root support carries an upper link and a forelink in series, and the forelink carries a compact end runner. The upper link and forelink pivot on two revolute joints, and the runner is a distal prismatic joint.

A ring-and-cradle pitch-roll head. A grounded base bracket carries an outer roll frame, and the roll frame carries an inner pitch cradle with a small output face. The outer frame rolls on a revolute joint about its main axis, and the inner cradle pitches on a perpendicular revolute joint.

A snap-lock utility knife with a sliding blade carriage and a thumb slider on top. The rigid handle body surrounds a central blade carrier, and the slider sits in a long guide slot along the spine. An undershot waterwheel. Two side frames support a horizontal axle carrying a paddle wheel below a simple channel or trough edge. Joints: the wheel rotates continuously about the axle axis. Use a taller support so the rotating stage sits high above the base.

A light missile launcher with two side-by-side tubes on an open frame pedestal. A passenger-car glove compartment with a bottom-hinged door and twin side stay arms. A countertop microwave with a side-hinged door and a row of membrane buttons. A rugged work headset with folding earcups, a padded headband, and a short boom mic. A bench module with a slide and a two-link arm. A fixed guide body supports a carriage-mounted shoulder block, then an upper link, then a short distal link with a square pad. category_title: Branching tree with three independent rotary branches
prompt: A metrology fixture tree with a rigid center column, one short branch near the base, one straight branch at mid-height, and one angled branch near the top. Each branch should terminate in a different pad or fork shape and should connect back to the column through its own bearing housing and side support geometry. The assembly should read as real industrial hardware with turned journals, machined side cheeks, stop lugs, and practical clearances around the rotating roots. The three branches are separate revolute joints about three clearly supported local hub axes mounted at staggered heights on the column. Keep every part mechanically grounded with no floating members, and make sure the articulation paths stay clear so nothing clips through covers, stops, housings, rails, or neighboring links.

A hinge-slide-hinge chain. A grounded base support carries a sliding middle member, and the far end of that member carries a small rotating fork or tab. The root is a revolute joint, the middle member is prismatic, and the tip tab is a second revolute joint.

A manual wheelchair. A chair frame supports two large rear wheels, two front casters, a seat, a backrest, and footrests. Joints: each rear wheel rotates continuously about its axle, and each front caster swivels about a vertical stem and spins continuously about its wheel axle. Keep the wheelbase short and the overall envelope compact.

A vacuum cleaner with an articulated wand. A main body connects to a rigid wand chain ending in a floor nozzle. Joints: the wand bends on one or two revolute elbows, and the floor nozzle pitches on a horizontal revolute hinge. Keep the links short and compact for a bench-scale footprint.

A open transfer gearbox with countershaft. A base frame carries an input shaft, a countershaft, and an output shaft, each with a simple spur gear and clear bearing blocks. Each shaft rotates on its own revolute joint about a supported axis.

A rotary table with tilting trunnion. A grounded base turntable supports two side cheeks, and a square or circular work table sits between the cheeks on a transverse shaft. The base rotates on a vertical revolute joint, and the work table tilts on a horizontal revolute joint carried by the side cheeks.

A compact service axis with a hinged extension nose. A fixed rail supports a moving carriage, the carriage carries a hinged frame, and the hinged frame contains a short rectangular ram. The carriage slides on a prismatic joint, the frame rotates on a revolute joint, and the ram slides on a distal prismatic joint.

An articulated TV wall mount shown without a screen. A grounded wall plate supports a folding two-link arm ending in a compact head frame with separate swivel and tilt supports. The arm has two folding revolute joints, and the head adds one swivel revolute about vertical and one tilt revolute about horizontal.

A package-delivery hand truck with a narrow frame and a folding upper handle loop. The wheels rotate on the lower axle, and the upper handle loop rotates on two small hinge pivots at the top of the frame. Keep the folding upper handle loop clipped to its top hinge pivots so it stays attached when folded flat.
A compact front-loader with a slim white body, a drum on a revolute axle, and a round door hinged on the right edge with a chrome latch handle.

A laundry center combo dryer section with a tall white rectangular body, a drum on a continuous revolute axle, a large porthole door on the left edge on two barrel revolute hinges, and a fold-down lint filter access flap on a revolute hinge at the lower body front face.

A retractable security bollard with a cylindrical post rising from a ground sleeve and a flange collar at the sleeve opening, plus a keyed top cap. the post stays visibly guided by the sleeve instead of floating above the pavement opening, and the cap is clearly separate from the post tube. The bollard post slides prismatically along the vertical axis inside the sleeve, and the keyed cap rotates continuously on its short local axis. Street-infrastructure scale.

A slim household tower fan with a narrow vertical body, a rounded base, and two knurled dials on the top cap, plus a separate oscillation button on the top deck. the front intake and outlet grille runs nearly the full height of the body, and the button is a distinct supported control beside the dials. The body oscillates left and right on a vertical joint at the base, the internal blower wheel spins continuously on the center axis, and the two dials rotate continuously, and the oscillation button translates independently as a small prismatic push control. A teaching-room drafting table with a wide floor base, a center column, and a tilting board with a bottom tool tray, plus a shallow accessory drawer under the board. the board support remains visibly separate from the height-adjustment column, and the drawer is carried by short runners below the board frame. The board tilts on a horizontal hinge at the support head and the column slides prismatically for height adjustment, and the accessory drawer slides prismatically on its guide runners. A wheelie bin with a top lid. A bin body sits on a rear axle with two wheels and carries one full-width lid along the back edge. Joints: the lid is revolute about a horizontal rear hinge axis, and the rear wheels spin continuously about the axle. Keep the body shallow and compact around the opening.
A carbon-fibre dropper seatpost with a lightweight outer tube, an inner post on a prismatic joint, and a low-profile inline saddle clamp.

A stool-height office chair with a tall gas column, a circular foot ring, and a small backrest. The five-star wheeled base supports the seat above the tall center post, and the foot ring is clamped to the post below the seat. The caster wheels spin on their axles, the caster forks swivel on vertical pivots, the seat swivels about the central post, the gas column slides vertically for height adjustment, and the foot ring slides vertically with its clamp on the post. A perforated rehearsal music stand with a flat plate base, a telescoping post, and a shallow punched-metal desk. A kitchen juicer with a compact base, a transparent upper lid, and a cylindrical feed pusher guided through the center of the lid. One side of the body has a hinged service door for access to the pulp channel, and a small control knob sits below it. The feed pusher slides prismatically in the chute, the lid rotates on a rear hinge, the service door rotates on a vertical side hinge, the control knob rotates on a short shaft, and the filter basket rotates continuously about the vertical axis.

category_title: Adjustable weight bench with hinged backrest
prompt: An adjustable decline bench with a long back pad, an articulated seat, and a front leg-roller assembly, plus a folding mid-frame brace below the seat section. the frame has a low wheel set at one end for moving the bench around the room, and the brace remains a distinct supported member in the frame. The back pad rotates on a central horizontal hinge, the seat rotates on its own hinge ahead of it, the paired leg rollers spin continuously on their support axle, and the transport wheels rotate continuously on the frame axle, and the brace rotates on its frame hinge as it tucks under the bench. A guyed derrick crane with a vertical mast, a diagonal boom arm braced by tension rod ties at the mast head, the boom pivoting on a horizontal revolute joint at the mast base, and a slewing revolute joint at the mast foot on a ground anchor plate.

category_title: Desktop pencil sharpener with hand crank and drawer
prompt: A premium desktop pencil sharpener with a rectangular body, a large side crank, a front drawer with a finger pull, and a circular point-adjustment dial around the pencil port. A three-carriage slide chain. A grounded outer guide carries a middle stage, the middle stage carries an inner stage, and the inner stage carries a short tip stage. The mechanism uses three serial prismatic joints.

category_title: Standing desk with synchronous telescoping legs and articulated controls
prompt: A compact home-office standing desk with a narrow top, twin telescoping legs, and a small side control pod under one corner, plus a shallow keyboard tray under the front edge. the top frame remains visibly separate from the lifting columns, and the tray is mounted on short side guides below the top frame. The two leg stages slide prismatically within their outer columns as a synchronized pair, and the keyboard tray slides prismatically on its guides. Small home-office desk scale.

A telescoping mast with pan head. Nested vertical mast sections rise from a grounded base, and the top of the mast carries a compact pan head with a simple faceplate. The mast sections are serial vertical prismatic joints, and the pan head is a revolute joint about the mast centerline at the top.

A rehabilitation overbed table with a U-shaped mobile base, a single mast, a split-top work surface, and a side tilt-release handle, plus a low foot-operated brake bar across the base. the smaller side wing shares the same support head as the main top instead of floating beside it, and the brake bar bridges the lower frame rails as a real supported part. The mast slides prismatically for height adjustment, the main top rotates on a horizontal hinge at the head, and the four base casters spin continuously on their axles, and the brake bar rotates on short pivots at the base frame. Therapy-room table scale.

A flatbed platform cart with a folding push handle and four swivel casters. The rectangular deck rides on a caster at each corner, and the push handle is hinged along one short edge of the platform. The handle rotates on a long hinge at the deck edge, and each caster wheel spins on its axle while the caster fork swivels on a vertical pivot.

A boxed palm two-finger chain set. A compact palm body carries two separate finger roots spaced apart on the front face, each root leading into a three-link phalanx chain. Each finger uses three serial revolute joints, and neither finger drives the other.

A clinical monocular microscope with a cast base, a vertical column, a monocular eyepiece tube, and a compact slide stage, plus a diaphragm lever under the stage. the focusing carriage and stage both remain visible as separate supported parts, and the lever is supported by the condenser housing instead of floating under the stage. The focusing carriage slides prismatically on the column, the turret rotates continuously on the nosepiece axis, and the stage moves prismatically front to back on its stage guide, and the diaphragm lever rotates on a short pivot beneath the stage. Bench-laboratory scale.

A USB drive with a swivel cover. A compact body carries the connector, and a U-shaped cover wraps around the body on a side pin. Joints: the cover rotates continuously about the side pivot axis. Use a cleaner silhouette with fewer secondary breaks around the rotating part.

A compact weapon station with a pan-tilt support and a side service panel. The lower pedestal supports a rotating upper body, and the weapon mount is carried on a short elevation yoke with a service panel in the side skin. The pedestal rotates on a vertical axis, the weapon mount pitches on a horizontal axis, and the side panel rotates on a small hinge.

A night-sign blimp with a rounded envelope, a short gondola, a side propeller nacelle on each side, and a cruciform tail with tall end fins, plus a small wheeled landing gear under the gondola. the nacelles stay visibly bolted or bracketed to the hull sides rather than floating beside it, and the gear remains a real supported undercarriage attached to the gondola structure. The propellers rotate continuously on their shafts, the rudder surfaces pivot on vertical hinges, and the elevator surfaces pivot on horizontal hinges, and the landing wheels rotate continuously on their axle. A portable rolling tool chest with a ribbed plastic shell, a telescoping pull handle at the back, and a hinged top lid over the main compartment, plus a shallow front drawer with a finger pull. the lower body stays deep enough to read as a real rolling chest rather than a suitcase, and the drawer remains aligned within the lower shell. The pull handle slides prismatically in its rear channels, the main lid rotates on a rear hinge line, and the two rear wheels spin continuously on their axle, and the drawer slides prismatically on straight guide runners. A rugged jobsite speaker with a boxy frame, a volume knob, and a folding handle. A compact mini-ITX graphics card with a single axial fan and a hinged anti-sag foot. The fan rotor spins continuously on its central axis, and the support foot rotates on a small hinge at the bracket edge.

A prismatic-revolute chain. A grounded base rail carries a sliding carriage, and the carriage supports a short hinged arm on a side clevis. The root motion is a prismatic joint along the rail, and the carried arm is a revolute joint about a supported pivot axis.

A slide-plunger with nose latch. A fixed guide tube carries a sliding plunger, and the plunger supports a small hinged nose latch at the tip. The plunger is a prismatic joint, and the nose latch is a carried revolute joint.

A column-mounted radial-arm module. A fixed post carries a rotary turret with a straight beam, and the beam supports a traveling truck on top or beneath it. The turret is a vertical revolute joint and the truck is a prismatic joint along the beam axis.

A serial elbow arm module. A grounded pedestal supports an upper arm link, a forearm link, and a compact end plate, with the shoulder and elbow hubs clearly carried by the fixed base and the upper arm. The shoulder and elbow are serial revolute joints about parallel horizontal axes.

A telescoping boom. Nested rectangular tube sections extend from a grounded root mount, with each inner section visibly captured by the one outside it. Each boom stage is a prismatic joint along the common boom axis.

A tilt-and-slide moonroof cassette with an aluminium frame, a glass panel that first tilts upward at the rear edge on a horizontal revolute hinge at the front frame rail, and then overrides to slide rearward on prismatic guide rails flush with the roof line.

A harbor lighthouse with a cylindrical lantern cage and an inner light carriage. The lantern room stands above a gallery, and the beacon carriage is supported on a central shaft inside the cage. A bifold louvered shutter with two narrow leaves joined at the center. A curbside mailbox with a front door and a raised side flag. The front door rotates on a vertical side hinge axis, and the signal flag rotates on its own small side pivot.

A tire swing suspended from a top beam by a short rigid yoke. The circular tire seat hangs below a central support block on the beam through two short rigid links. The yoke rotates on the beam pivot, and the tire seat rotates on its hanger pivots beneath the yoke.

A spice-carousel lazy Susan with a tall outer rail and a central bearing base. The round tray has shallow compartments around the perimeter and a low circular base ring beneath. The tray rotates continuously about the vertical center axis at the bearing base.

A stairclimber scooter with a flat deck, front and rear primary wheels on revolute axles, and two auxiliary stair-assist wheels at the deck rear corners each on a revolute axle, folding on revolute hinges at the deck underside corner mounts when not in use.

A locking cat flap with a side sliding security panel. The fixed frame surrounds the top-hinged flap panel, and a narrow lockout panel slides in guides along one side of the opening. The flap rotates on a horizontal upper hinge axis, and the security panel slides prismatically in its side guides.

A portal gantry with vertical slide. Two grounded uprights and a top beam form the fixed frame, and a centered beam carriage supports a short Z slide beneath it. A wall-mount router with a front service cover over the ports. The slim rectangular body has a hinged lower cover panel and two short antennas projecting from the top edge. A side-swing pickup tailgate with a spare-tire cutout and a latch lever. The gate panel is mounted to one rear bed corner on vertical hinges and closes against the opposite corner with a lever latch. A bench-top three-joint revolute chain. A base foot supports a stack of slim links with clear side plates at the joints, and the distal link ends in a compact end tab. There are three revolute joints in sequence, each supported by a local hinge block and aligned to the same motion plane.

A wheelbarrow. A tray sits between two handles, with one front wheel in a fork and two resting legs at the rear. Joints: the front wheel rotates continuously about its axle. Place the wheels close to the body so the silhouette stays tight.

A bench-top folding arm chain. A base foot supports a stack of slim links with clear side plates at the joints, and the distal link ends in a compact end pad. There are three revolute joints in sequence, each supported by a local hinge block and aligned to the same motion plane.

A wall-mounted medicine cabinet with a mirrored front door. The shallow rectangular case is fixed to the wall, and the mirrored door is attached to one side on two compact hinges. A low-profile carriage carrying an elbow arm. A broad fixed rail supports a moving block, and the block carries two hinged links sized so the root stage still reads as the larger positioning system. The rail stage is prismatic, and the two carried joints are revolute shoulder and elbow joints.

category_title: Branching tree with three independent rotary branches

A compact hinge-slide-hinge mechanism. A fixed base clevis supports a carried slider body, and the slider body supports a small output tab at its far end. The chain is a root revolute joint followed by a prismatic stage and a distal revolute joint.

A manual wheelchair. A chair frame supports two large rear wheels, two front casters, a seat, a backrest, and footrests. A vacuum cleaner with an articulated wand. A main body connects to a rigid wand chain ending in a floor nozzle. Joints: the wand bends on one or two revolute elbows, and the floor nozzle pitches on a horizontal revolute hinge. Favor boxy joint housings and thick links over thin shells.

A right-angle gear assembly. A base bracket supports a vertical shaft with a bevel gear and a horizontal shaft with the mating bevel gear, plus clear bearing blocks on both shafts. The vertical and horizontal shafts are separate revolute joints about their own supported axes.

A pedestal-mounted trunnion positioner. A fixed foot supports a rotating lower platform, and the lower platform carries a pair of trunnion supports holding a compact faceplate. The lower platform and upper faceplate are separate revolute joints on vertical and horizontal axes.

A wall-backed transfer unit with a hinged nose stage. A fixed back frame carries a slide carriage and a compact pivot bracket, while the bracket supports a smaller sliding output member. The chain consists of one prismatic joint, one revolute joint, and one distal prismatic joint.

A service-bay TV wall bracket. A grounded wall mount carries a stout primary arm, a slimmer secondary arm, and a rectangular mounting frame with visible pan-and-tilt support. An appliance hand truck with a tall frame and a folding toe plate. The tubular upright carries two large rear wheels, and the broad lower nose plate is hinged so it can fold flat against the frame. A removable-access bollard with a stout cylindrical post, a recessed sleeve, and a hinged lock cover beside the opening, plus a keyed top cap. the lock cover is mounted to the sleeve rim as a separate supported flap, and the cap is clearly separate from the post tube. A bedroom tower fan with a tall body shell, a low oval base, and a top-mounted timer knob beside a speed dial, plus a separate oscillation button on the top deck. the control deck remains a visibly separate top surface rather than a featureless cap, and the button is a distinct supported control beside the dials. A floor-standing drafting table with a twin-post base, a central board support, and a broad drawing surface, plus a shallow accessory drawer under the board. the twin posts clearly carry the same rigid board assembly together, and the drawer is carried by short runners below the board frame. A two-bolt collar dropper seatpost with saddle and a straight outer tube clamped by a standard seat tube binder ring, an inner telescoping post on a prismatic joint, a lever-actuated locking collar around the inner post, and a two-bolt saddle rail clamp plate at the post top.

A drawer-like shuttle with a pivoting paddle. A grounded slide channel carries a moving block, and the block supports a short output plate on a hinge at one side. The shuttle is a prismatic joint and the paddle is a revolute joint on the shuttle.

A service latch-style plunger assembly. A grounded sleeve supports a sliding plunger, and the plunger nose meets a short output lever mounted in a fork at the front. The sleeve and plunger form a prismatic joint, while the output lever is a revolute joint on the fork.

A compact service arm with a sliding head. A fixed base support carries a single rotary arm, and the outer portion of that arm supports a short sliding carriage. The root support provides one revolute joint and the head carriage provides one prismatic joint.

An open-frame elbow arm fixture. A grounded stand supports two articulated links with exposed pin bosses and a simple end-effector plate. The shoulder joint is a revolute at the stand, and the elbow joint is a second revolute between the two links.

A wall-backed telescoping reach module. A rigid rear mount carries an outer sleeve and successive inner telescoping sections, with clear overlap remaining between sections. The telescoping sections are serial prismatic joints along the shared axis.

A spoiler sunroof cassette with a rectangular aluminium frame, a glass panel that lifts at the rear edge on a horizontal revolute hinge at the front frame rail and simultaneously slides rearward on twin prismatic guide rails with rolling shoe carriages at the panel rear corners.

A squat harbor beacon tower with an exposed turntable lantern and a side equipment door. An exterior storm shutter with one large louvered panel and a hold-open arm. A wide bench playground swing with side suspension arms. The bench seat hangs from two rigid side arms connected to the playground crossbeam above. The bench rotates on the upper hanger pivots at the crossbeam and on the lower pivots at the seat sides.

A condiment lazy Susan with a raised handle arch over the center. The circular tray is mounted on a low turntable base, and the arch handle is fixed across the top of the rotating tray. The tray rotates continuously about the vertical center axis at the base bearing.

A pet door built into a side-hinged panel door. The outer human-sized door is fixed here as the support frame, and the small pet flap is mounted near its lower section on a top hinge. The pet flap rotates on a horizontal upper hinge axis within the larger door panel.

A wide inspection portal. Box-section side frames support a rigid crossbeam, with a small shuttle under the beam and a narrow tool carriage below the shuttle. The shuttle slides prismatically across the beam and the tool carriage slides prismatically in the vertical direction.

A home mesh router with a top vent panel and a folding setup flap. The compact tower body has a small rear access flap near the base and a round setup dial on the top face. The access flap rotates on a small hinge at the back, and the setup dial rotates on its own vertical axis on the top panel.

A multi-function tailgate with a split center door and a lower drop panel. A stationary exercise bike. A floor frame supports the flywheel housing, crank set, seat post, and handlebar mast. A tubular steel side-yard gate with a support wheel under the free end. A boxed industrial three-joint revolute chain. A reinforced base cheek supports deeper-section links with visible pin bosses, and the final link terminates in a compact end tab. The assembly is a serial chain of three revolute joints about parallel hinge axes.

A side-post cantilever arm. A grounded post supports a shoulder link, a forelink, and a short wrist face, with the chain projecting entirely to one side of the support. The shoulder, elbow, and wrist are serial revolute joints carried by successive members.

A broad-knuckle finger linkage. A fixed base tab supports a proximal phalanx, a middle phalanx, and a distal phalanx with believable overlap at each hinge. The three phalanx segments are connected by three revolute joints about parallel knuckle axes.

A ladder-frame four-joint revolute chain. A grounded root bracket carries five rigid links in series with long side plates and a compact end tab at the tip. The chain uses four serial revolute joints about parallel axes in one plane.

A wheelbarrow. A tray sits between two handles, with one front wheel in a fork and two resting legs at the rear. Joints: the front wheel rotates continuously about its axle. Offset the upper support structure slightly forward of the wheel centers.

A side-plate folding arm chain. A fixed cheek supports a sequence of flat-sided links with compact pin bosses, ending in a small end pad. The links are joined by three revolute joints on parallel supported axes.

A service module combining a hinge and an extension stage. A fixed support carries one rigid arm link, and the arm carries a guided output member that slides along the link centerline. The motion order is revolute at the base and prismatic at the distal extension.

A low-profile vertical slide carrying a hinged bracket. A broad lower mount supports a vertical guide stage and a small forward-projecting hinged face on the moving block. The moving block is a vertical prismatic joint and the bracket is a revolute joint carried by that block.

A glass-front display cabinet with two narrow framed doors. The rigid case carries glazed doors on side hinges, and each door has a small round pull knob near the meeting stile. The two doors rotate on vertical hinge axes at the side walls, and the pull knobs rotate on their local axes.
