A boxed industrial multi-segment fold-out arm. A reinforced base cheek supports deeper-section links with visible pin bosses, and the final link terminates in a small platform bracket. The assembly is a serial chain of four revolute joints about parallel hinge axes.

A wall-backed three-joint arm. A reinforced backplate supports a projecting shoulder block, followed by a deeper upper arm, a forearm, and a short wrist flange. The backplate grounds the shoulder revolute, the upper arm carries the elbow revolute, and the forearm carries the wrist revolute.

A pair of folding reading glasses with a center bridge hinge. The two half-fronts meet at a hinged bridge, and each half-front carries a short folding temple at its outer edge. An overshot wheel on a timber frame with a side service hatch near the axle support. category_title: Branching tree with two independent rotary branches

A saxophone case with an asymmetrical molded lower shell and a fitted top lid. A boxed industrial lever chain. A reinforced base cheek supports deeper-section links with visible pin bosses, and the final link terminates in a small end tab. The assembly is a serial chain of three revolute joints about parallel hinge axes.

A shoulder-elbow-extension chain. A grounded base bracket supports two hinged links, and the distal link supports a short slider at its tip. The shoulder and elbow are revolute joints, and the tip stage is a prismatic joint.

A trunnion-and-spindle pitch-roll unit. A side-supported pitch frame carries a centered roll spindle with a short front face. Pitch is a revolute joint about the frame trunnions, and roll is a separate revolute joint about the spindle axis.

A slim utility knife with a top thumb slider and a side safety latch. The rigid handle shell surrounds the blade track and carries a small latch tab in the side wall. An undershot waterwheel. Two side frames support a horizontal axle carrying a paddle wheel below a simple channel or trough edge. Joints: the wheel rotates continuously about the axle axis. Use paired side supports framing the rotating stage.

A slide-shoulder-elbow chain. A grounded base slide carries a moving carriage, the carriage carries a shoulder link, and the shoulder link carries a forelink. The root is a prismatic joint followed by shoulder and elbow revolute joints.

category_title: Branching tree with three independent rotary branches

A low-profile rotary-slide-rotary assembly. A grounded base supports one hinged body, the hinged body supports a small linear stage, and the linear stage supports a short end fork. The motion order is revolute, prismatic, then revolute.

A bevel-to-spur transfer assembly. A grounded base holds a vertical input shaft that turns through a bevel pair into a horizontal shaft which then carries a spur gear stage. The shafts on both stages are separate revolute joints on clearly supported axes.

An inspection rig for a prismatic-revolute-prismatic chain. A fixed base, a moving carriage, a pivot frame, and a small telescoping nose are arranged so each support system stays explicit. The motion order is prismatic at the base, revolute at the hinge frame, and prismatic at the terminal slider.

A heavy-duty wall display mount shown bare. A reinforced wall plate supports a two-link arm and a square head frame with a tilting crossbar, making each support stage explicit. The arm folds through two revolute joints, and the head frame uses separate swivel and tilt revolute joints.

A folding luggage hand truck with telescoping handle rails. The compact frame has two inset wheels, a hinged toe plate at the bottom, and nested handle tubes that rise from the side rails. A washer with a top-access service panel — a white rectangular body, a drum on a continuous revolute axle, a circular porthole door on the left edge on two barrel revolute knuckle hinges, and a hinged top service panel on two rear revolute hinge pins for access to the control board.

A compact drafting stand with a pedestal base, a telescoping center post, and a medium rectangular board with a lower lip, plus a shallow accessory drawer under the board. the telescoping post remains visibly nested rather than reading as one solid mast, and the drawer is carried by short runners below the board frame. A short-travel trail dropper seatpost with a round outer tube, a short inner post tube, and a saddle clamp at the top. The inner post slides prismatically inside the outer tube for drop travel. A saddle rail clamp at the top adjusts the saddle angle on a revolute hinge.
